categories.name: Linear Algebra
categories.description: Choose one linear algebra course. AM 10 is Engineering Math Methods I.

categories.name: Differential Equations
categories.description: Choose one differential equations course. AM 20 is Engineering Math Methods II.

categories.name: Multivariable Calculus
categories.description: Choose one multivariable/vector calculus course. AM 30 is Multivariate Calculus for Engineers.

categories.name: Physics
categories.description: Required physics sequence: Mechanics, Waves & Optics, Electricity & Magnetism.

categories.name: Statics and Mechanics of Materials
categories.description: Required statics and mechanics of materials course.

categories.name: Signals & Systems
categories.description: Required signals and systems course with lab.

categories.name: Lower Division Programming Core
categories.description: Required lower-division programming and computer systems courses.

categories.name: Systems & C Programming
categories.description: Choose one systems and C programming course. CSE 13S is strongly recommended, with exception to those pursuing a Digital Hardware Concentration.

categories.name: Upper Division Core
categories.description: Required upper-division core courses: Logic Design, Data Structures & Algorithms, Computer Architecture, Embedded System Design, Electronic Circuits.

categories.name: Concentration (choose one)
categories.description: Choose one concentration. System Programming: CSE 130, CSE 150, CSE 111 or CSE 134, CSE 125 (CSE 225 substitutable), plus one of CSE 113/CSE 156L/CSE 110A. Computer Systems: CSE 130, CSE 111 or CSE 134, CSE 122 (CSE 222A substitutable), CSE 220. Networks: CSE 130, CSE 150, CSE 156/L, CSE 122 (CSE 222A substitutable), plus CSE 122 or CSE 228A. Digital Hardware: CSE 125 (CSE 225 substitutable), ECE 171/L or CSE 122, plus one of CSE 122/CSE 220/CSE 228A/ECE 173.

categories.name: Disciplinary Communication (DC)
categories.description: DC requirement. CSE 185E is Technical Writing. CSE 195 is Senior Thesis (also satisfies capstone).

categories.name: Capstone
categories.description: Choose one capstone option. CSE 123A/B requires CSE 121 and prior/concurrent CSE 185E. CSE 127A/B requires CSE 125 or 225, prior/concurrent CSE 185E, and prior/concurrent CSE 122 or 222A. ECE 118 requires ECE 101/L, CSE 100/L, and ECE 13; satisfies DC requirement. ECE 118 cannot be used as both elective and capstone. CSE 195 requires submission of an approved senior thesis and also satisfies DC.

categories.name: Upper Division Electives
categories.description: Choose elective courses from the Computer Engineering Elective list on the Undergraduate Advising website. Each concentration requires 1-2 electives from the approved list. Digital Hardware concentration may also choose from the approved Digital Hardware Grad-Level Course List. See: https://undergrad.engineering.ucsc.edu/curriculum-charts/
